posture: Quiet Monday scan — no confirmable new, in-scope innovation signal surfaced in the last 24 hours. The sector's active frontiers (FOGO/circular economy, AI-assisted development assessment, smart communities) remain warm but produced no fresh, in-window council announcement today; the strongest recent 'adopt' material remains the 2026 National Awards for Local Government already logged on 29 June.
summary: Empty day. A full daily scan of the free §1a-§1d sources (AU council & sector press, AU industry verticals, overseas equal-weight outlets and the tertiary/research institutes) returned no item that was simultaneously (a) published in the last 24 hours, (b) in scope per the §3b Australian-council-function filter, and (c) not already covered in the previous 3 days. Today is a Monday, so the §1e 50-council watchlist deep scan (Saturdays only) was not run. Several recurring or undated items were again considered and excluded: the NSW commercial FOGO mandate (business-tier obligations commenced 1 July) is a regulatory-rollout milestone, not a fresh innovation signal; the ACT Belconnen/Tuggeranong FOGO multi-unit pilot resurfaced only via undated rollout coverage; Hawkesbury City Council's black-soldier-fly waste-to-resource trial dates to earlier 2026; recycled-glass road decontamination facilities at Bendigo/Wangaratta surfaced via older coverage; the NSW AI-in-planning tools (Early Adopter Grant Program, DAISY, AI Solutions Panel) produced no new dated development; and overseas material (Bloomberg CityLab 2026, LGIU AI/waste-reform briefings, Centerville Ohio AI recycling-contamination camera) was either out-of-window, general policy, or already reviewed. Defence and police-surveillance drone items were excluded as out-of-scope per §3b (not AU council functions). No paywalled items were logged; where a paid title was the only source it was held to headline + standfirst and not used. Sections are published empty per §6.
